title: Education Record Privacy
description: Maintain confidentiality of student education records per FERPA with annual notification of rights.

title: Directory Information Opt-Out
description: Provide annual opportunity for parents/students to opt out of directory information disclosure.

title: Written Consent for Disclosure
description: Obtain prior written consent before disclosing PII from education records to third parties.

title: School Official Exception
description: Document legitimate educational interest before disclosure under school official exception.

title: Right to Inspect and Review
description: Provide parents/eligible students access to education records within 45 days of request.

title: Right to Amend Records
description: Allow parents/eligible students to request amendment of inaccurate or misleading records.

title: Disclosure Recordkeeping
description: Maintain records of disclosures of education records for audit per FERPA.

title: Records Transfer to Other Schools
description: Document records transfer to schools where student seeks enrollment per FERPA exception.

title: Studies Exception Documentation
description: Document IRB-approved studies disclosure with data security safeguards per FERPA studies exception.

title: Law Enforcement Unit Records
description: Maintain separation between law enforcement records and education records.

title: Verifiable Parental Consent
description: Obtain verifiable parental consent before collecting personal info from children under 13.

title: Children's Privacy Notice
description: Provide direct notice to parents about info collection practices regarding their children.

title: Parental Access and Deletion
description: Provide parents with means to review and delete children's personal information.

title: Data Retention Limits
description: Retain personal info from children only as long as reasonably necessary.

title: Reasonable Security Measures
description: Establish reasonable procedures to protect confidentiality of children's personal info.

title: School Authorization for Edtech
description: Allow schools to consent on behalf of parents for educational services collecting student data.

title: Lawful Basis for Student Data
description: Document lawful basis (consent or task carried out in public interest) for student data processing.

title: Article 8 Children's Consent
description: Apply parental consent for under-16 (or member-state lower limit, min 13) per GDPR Article 8.

title: Right of Access for Students
description: Respond to student/parent data subject access requests within one month.

title: Right to Erasure
description: Honor right to erasure subject to legitimate retention for academic record obligations.

title: 72-Hour Breach Notification
description: Notify supervisory authority of personal data breach within 72 hours.

title: Data Protection by Design
description: Implement appropriate technical and organizational measures by design and default.

title: DPIA for Large-Scale Student Processing
description: Conduct DPIA for systematic monitoring or profiling of student behavior.

title: DPO Appointment for Schools
description: Appoint DPO for educational institutions involving large-scale processing of student data.

title: International Student Data Transfers
description: Use SCCs or adequacy decisions for cross-border student data transfers.

title: ADA Title II Accessibility
description: Ensure educational programs and digital materials are accessible per ADA Title II.

title: ADA Title III for Private Schools
description: Meet ADA Title III public accommodations for private educational institutions.

title: Section 504 Accommodations
description: Provide accommodations to students with disabilities per Section 504 of Rehabilitation Act.

title: Section 508 IT Accessibility
description: Federal IT systems and educational tech procurement meet Section 508 standards.

title: WCAG 2.1 Level AA
description: Meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility for student-facing portals and learning content.

title: IEP Development and Implementation
description: Develop and implement Individualized Education Programs per IDEA Part B.

title: Procedural Safeguards
description: Provide procedural safeguards notice to parents per IDEA.

title: Confidentiality of IDEA Records
description: Protect confidentiality of personally identifiable information in IDEA records.

title: Title IX Non-Discrimination
description: Prohibit sex-based discrimination in federally funded education programs.

title: Title IX Coordinator Designation
description: Designate Title IX coordinator and post contact information per regulations.

title: Clery Act Annual Security Report
description: Publish annual security report and crime statistics per Clery Act.

title: Timely Warning Notification
description: Issue timely warnings for crimes posing serious or continuing threat to campus safety.

title: Title IV Aid Administration
description: Administer Title IV student aid per Higher Education Act and Federal Student Aid Handbook.

title: Consumer Information Disclosure
description: Disclose consumer information including completion rates per HEA.

title: P2P File Sharing Policy
description: Implement plan to combat unauthorized P2P distribution of copyrighted material.

title: Master Promissory Note
description: Use compliant Master Promissory Note for federal student loans.

title: PPRA Survey Restrictions
description: Apply Protection of Pupil Rights Amendment for surveys revealing protected info.

title: GLBA Safeguards for Financial Aid
description: Apply GLBA Safeguards Rule to student financial aid records.

title: FCRA for Educational Credit
description: Apply FCRA to credit decisions affecting student loan and financial aid.

title: TCPA for Auto-Dial Outreach
description: Comply with TCPA before automated SMS or call outreach to students.

title: CAN-SPAM for Education Email
description: Comply with CAN-SPAM Act for educational marketing email.

title: Security Trust Services Criteria
description: Implement controls aligned with AICPA TSC Common Criteria for security.

title: Confidentiality Controls for Edu
description: Identify, classify, and protect confidential student information.

title: Role-Based Access for Schools
description: Implement RBAC with periodic review for educator and student accounts.

title: Continuous Monitoring of Edu Systems
description: Continuously monitor education system access with automated alerting.

title: Education-Aware ISMS
description: Tailor ISMS scope to education-specific information assets.

title: Student Data Encryption
description: Apply ISO 27001 cryptographic controls to student PII at rest and in transit.

title: Asset Inventory for Edu
description: Maintain inventory of student information assets with classification.

title: Edtech Vendor Information Security
description: Address information security in edtech vendor agreements with monitoring.

title: Cloud PII Protection
description: Apply ISO 27018 PII protection in cloud edtech acting as PII processors.

title: PECR Marketing Consent
description: Obtain consent before marketing electronic communications per ePrivacy.

title: Cookie Consent for Trackers
description: Obtain explicit consent for non-essential cookies and tracking technologies.

title: California Consumer Privacy Rights
description: Honor CCPA rights for California education consumer/parent data.

title: CPRA Sensitive Personal Information
description: Honor CPRA right to limit use of sensitive personal information about students.

title: California Student Online Privacy Act
description: Comply with California SOPIPA for student data processed by online service operators.

title: EU AI Act for Educational AI
description: Classify educational AI systems per EU AI Act with conformity assessment for high-risk uses.

title: High-Risk Educational AI
description: Classify student assessment and admission AI as high-risk per EU AI Act Annex III.

title: FTC Section 5 Compliance
description: Avoid unfair or deceptive practices in education service marketing.

title: State Authorization for Distance Education
description: Obtain authorization in each state where students are enrolled in distance education.

title: Recognized Accreditor Status
description: Maintain recognized accreditor status for Title IV eligibility.

title: Edtech School Official Designation
description: Designate edtech vendors as school officials with legitimate educational interest documentation.

title: Vendor Data Use Restrictions
description: Limit vendor use of student data to school-authorized purposes per FERPA.

title: ESSA Compliance
description: Comply with Every Student Succeeds Act for federally funded K-12 programs.

title: FedRAMP Authorization for Federal Edu
description: Maintain FedRAMP authorization for federal education cloud services.

title: Automated Grading Decisions
description: Provide right not to be subject to solely automated grading decisions with legal effects.

title: Scientific Research Exception
description: Apply GDPR Article 89 safeguards when processing student data for educational research.

title: Biometric Data in Schools
description: Apply FERPA protections to biometric records (fingerprint, retinal, voice).

title: Illinois Biometric Privacy
description: Obtain written consent before collecting biometric identifiers in IL schools.

title: Student Privacy Pledge Compliance
description: Adhere to Student Privacy Pledge commitments for student data handling.

title: Classroom Video Records
description: Apply FERPA to video recordings of identifiable students in classrooms.

title: Deceased Student Records
description: Apply FERPA after student death only if state law extends rights.

title: Robocall Restrictions for Schools
description: Apply TCPA exemptions for emergency school robocalls and obtain consent for non-emergency.

title: Education Cybersecurity Best Practices
description: Apply CISA K-12 cybersecurity guidance for school cybersecurity programs.

title: NIST CSF for Education
description: Adopt NIST Cybersecurity Framework for education sector implementation.

title: Open Educational Resources
description: Comply with EU Open Data Directive for publicly funded research outputs.

title: Joint Controller Arrangements
description: Document joint controller arrangements between schools and edtech vendors.

title: Article 28 Edtech DPA
description: Execute Article 28-compliant data processing agreements with edtech processors.

title: Metadata as Education Records
description: Treat metadata identifying students as part of education records under FERPA.

title: California Minor Eraser Law
description: Allow California minors to remove content they posted under SB 568.

title: State-Specific Cybersecurity Laws
description: Comply with state-specific cybersecurity laws for student data (e.g., NYS Education Law 2-d).

title: Closed Captioning Requirement
description: Provide closed captioning for video content in education per ADA effective communication.

title: Auxiliary Aids and Services
description: Provide auxiliary aids/services for students with disabilities per Section 504.

title: Drug-Free Schools Compliance
description: Implement Drug-Free Schools and Communities Act program annually.

title: Selective Service Registration
description: Verify Selective Service registration status for federal aid eligibility (where required).

title: VA Education Benefits Compliance
description: Comply with VA principles of excellence for institutions enrolling veterans.

title: Directory Information Definition
description: Define directory information categories in annual FERPA notification.

title: Campus Partner Disclosure
description: Document disclosures to school officials with legitimate educational interest.

title: Subpoena Compliance
description: Notify parents/students before disclosing records pursuant to subpoena.

title: Health and Safety Emergency
description: Disclose education records in articulable health/safety emergency without consent.

title: Properly De-Identified Data
description: Apply 34 CFR 99.31(b) standards before releasing de-identified student data.

title: DOJ ADA Title II Web Rules
description: Comply with DOJ ADA Title II web accessibility rule effective 2026 for state/local govt.

title: European Accessibility Act for Edu
description: Comply with European Accessibility Act for educational products and services.

title: Education Cyber Insurance
description: Maintain cyber insurance with documented control attestations.

title: Education Business Continuity
description: Establish BCMS for school continuity during disruptions per ISO 22301.

title: Web Accessibility Audit
description: Conduct quarterly accessibility audits using automated and manual testing.
